steps.1: Select the current infection status of the patient. A confirmed case has tested positive for the pathogen. A suspected case shows symptoms but is still awaiting test results. A contact patient has been in close contact with an infected person but shows no symptoms.
steps.2: Select the type of pathogen involved. MRSA and similar multi-resistant bacteria, 3MRGN/4MRGN/VRE, and C. difficile each require different isolation and hygiene measures.
steps.3: Indicate where on or in the body the pathogen has been detected. The location determines which body areas require extra protection during contact — for example, airway pathogens require respiratory protection.
steps.4: Describe the type of interaction or procedure planned with this patient. The required protective equipment (gloves, gown, mask) depends heavily on how close and invasive the contact will be.
steps.5: Note any factors that may increase the risk of pathogen transmission. Coughing can spread respiratory pathogens, incontinence increases contact risk, and dementia may prevent the patient from cooperating with hygiene measures.

chat: Based on the patient information you provided, the Hygiene Assistant can now answer specific questions about required protective measures, isolation protocols, or other hygiene-related topics. You can ask follow-up questions at any time.
